CREATE TABLE time_series (value Float64, time DateTime);
SELECT time, value, exponentialTimeDecayedSum(1)(value, time) OVER (ORDER BY time) AS exp_avg FROM time_series;
CREATE TABLE second_series (value Float64, time DateTime);

SELECT t1.time, t1.value, t2.value, exponentialTimeDecayedSum(5)(t1.value, t1.time) OVER (ORDER BY t1.time) AS exp_avg, exponentialTimeDecayedSum(2)(t2.value, t2.time) OVER (ORDER BY t2.time) AS exp_avg_2 FROM time_series AS t1 JOIN second_series AS t2 ON t1.time < t2.time;
SELECT number, value, exponentialTimeDecayedSum(10)(value, number) OVER (ORDER BY number) AS exp_smooth FROM (SELECT number, (number % 5) AS value FROM numbers(100));
CREATE TABLE aggregated_series (date Date, value Float64);
SELECT date, exponentialTimeDecayedSum(1)(value, date) OVER (ORDER BY date) AS exp_avg FROM aggregated_series;
CREATE TABLE complex_series (time DateTime, value Float64);
SELECT time, value, exponentialTimeDecayedSum(5)(IFNULL(value, 0), time) OVER (ORDER BY time) AS exp_avg FROM complex_series;
